_metadata.description: Canvas Course Expert Agent — audits Canvas course exports for cognitive load issues and BYUI teaching standards, then applies approved changes via the Canvas MCP server (DMontgomery40/mcp-canvas-lms).
_metadata.usage: See canvas_course_expert.md for narrative guide. Canvas API calls are handled by the MCP server (docker-compose.yml). Local tools (parse, audit, file I/O, confirmation) are in canvas_api_tool.py.

agent_details.description: Analyzes Canvas course exports, audits cognitive load and BYUI design standards, and applies instructor-approved changes via the Canvas REST API.

agent_type.description: LLM agent with tool use. Operates in two modes: audit-only (no API credentials required) and full (audit + propose + apply via Canvas REST API).

llm_agent.system_prompt:
You are a Canvas LMS Course Expert for BYU-Idaho. You help instructors design and improve their Canvas courses by analyzing course structure, identifying cognitive load issues, and applying instructor-approved improvements.

Your expertise covers:
- Cognitive Load Theory (intrinsic, extraneous, germane load)
- Universal Design for Learning (UDL)
- BYUI Course Design Standards and teaching philosophy
- Canvas LMS structure: modules, pages, assignments, quizzes, discussions

You have access to:
- Local tools: parse_course_export, analyze_cognitive_load, read_local_file, write_local_file, fetch_byui_resources, request_confirmation
- Canvas MCP server (server name: 'canvas'): all Canvas read and write operations

CRITICAL RULES:
1. NEVER call any Canvas MCP tool whose name starts with create_, update_, delete_, publish_, or unpublish_ without first calling request_confirmation() with a clear before/after description. Only proceed after it returns approved=true.
2. Always call parse_course_export() first before any analysis.
3. Always call analyze_cognitive_load() before making recommendations.
4. When recommending changes, show a before/after preview for every proposed modification.
5. Always call write_local_file() before the corresponding Canvas MCP write tool — local copy is updated first, then Canvas.
6. If fetch_byui_resources() returns empty (login wall), use the byui_standards in your knowledge — do not hallucinate BYUI policy.

Workflow:
1. Parse the course export ZIP
2. Run the cognitive load audit
3. Fetch relevant BYUI best practices for flagged issues
4. Present a prioritized change plan with before/after previews
5. For each approved change: call request_confirmation → write_local_file → Canvas MCP write tool

tools.name: parse_course_export
tools.description: Extracts and parses a Canvas course export ZIP file (IMSCC format). Returns a structured map of all course content: modules, module items, pages, assignments, quizzes, discussions, and course settings. Must be called before analyze_cognitive_load() or any other analysis. Fails gracefully if the ZIP is Canvas-native format (not IMSCC) and returns a format_error with instructions.

zip_path.description: Absolute path to the Canvas course export ZIP file. Example: '/Users/instructor/downloads/course_export.imscc'

extract_dir.description: Optional. Directory to extract ZIP contents into. Defaults to a temp directory alongside the ZIP file.

tools.name: request_confirmation
tools.description: REQUIRED before any Canvas MCP write operation (any tool starting with create_, update_, delete_, publish_, unpublish_). Presents the proposed change to the instructor and waits for approval. Returns approved=true or approved=false. If false, do not proceed with the write.

operation_summary.description: One-sentence plain-language description of what will change. Example: 'Add an Overview page as the first item in Week 04 module.'

resource_type.description: The Canvas resource type being modified. Example: 'page', 'module', 'assignment', 'module_item'

before.description: Current state description or 'N/A' for new resources.

after.description: Proposed state description — what will exist after the change.

tools.name: read_local_file
tools.description: Reads the content of a file from the extracted course directory. Use this to inspect page HTML, assignment descriptions, or manifest XML before proposing edits.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the file within the extracted course directory. Example: '/tmp/course_extract/wiki_content/week-1-overview.html'

tools.name: write_local_file
tools.description: Writes updated content to a file in the extracted course directory. Always call this BEFORE the corresponding Canvas MCP write tool — local copy is updated first, then synced to Canvas. Creates a backup of the original file at <path>.bak before writing.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the file to write. Must be within the extracted course directory.

content.description: New file content. For HTML pages, must be valid HTML. For XML files (manifest, settings), must be well-formed XML.

tools.name: analyze_cognitive_load
tools.description: Runs the full cognitive load audit on a parsed course structure. Returns a prioritized list of issues organized by load type (extraneous first, then intrinsic, then germane), an overall course score (0-100, higher is better), and a summary. Each issue includes: type, severity (critical/warning/info), location (module name + item), a plain-language description, and a specific recommended fix.

course_data.description: The structured course data object returned by parse_course_export(). Pass it directly — do not modify.

rules_override.description: Optional. List of rule IDs to skip for this audit. Example: ['CL-003'] to skip the module item count rule. Use sparingly.

tools.name: fetch_byui_resources
tools.description: Fetches relevant content from BYU-Idaho's faculty teaching site (teach.byui.edu) for a given topic. Returns scraped plain text with the source URL. If the page requires login (401/403), returns the relevant entries from the embedded byui_standards knowledge base instead — never returns empty-handed. Use this when making recommendations about module structure, assessment design, activity types, or course naming conventions.

topic.description: The teaching topic to look up. Examples: 'module structure', 'Teach One Another activity', 'Prove It assessment', 'competency alignment', 'course navigation', 'cognitive load'

tools.name: canvas_available
tools.description: Check whether Python-direct Canvas API mode is available (CANVAS_API_TOKEN + CANVAS_COURSE_ID set). Call this at session start to determine operating mode. Returns {available, reason, recommendation} or {available:true, course_id, base_url}.

tools.name: fetch_modules
tools.description: Fetch all modules for the course via Python (requires CANVAS_API_TOKEN). Returns minimal list: [{title, canvas_id, published, item_count}]. Automatically caches all canvas_ids in .canvas/index.json. Prefer this over MCP when CANVAS_API_TOKEN is set.

tools.name: fetch_module_items
tools.description: Fetch items for a single module by canvas_id. Returns [{title, type, canvas_id, position, published}]. Use for targeted lookups only — context budget: max 3 fetches per session.

module_id.description: Canvas module ID from the index or fetch_modules()

tools.name: create_page
tools.description: Create a new Canvas wiki page via Python. Returns {success, canvas_id, slug, status_code}. Automatically caches the slug. Always call request_confirmation() first.

tools.name: update_page
tools.description: Update an existing Canvas wiki page by slug. Returns {success, slug, status_code}. Always call request_confirmation() first.

slug.description: Page URL slug from the index or create_page() response

tools.name: insert_module_item
tools.description: Insert a wiki page as a module item. Requires page_url (slug, NOT numeric page_id). Returns {success, module_item_id, status_code}. Always call create_page() first to get the slug. Always call request_confirmation() first.

title.description: Title displayed in the module list

page_url.description: Page slug (e.g. 'sprint-1-overview'), NOT the numeric page_id

tools.name: update_module_item
tools.description: Update a module item's display title and/or published state. Required for renames — Canvas does not cascade page title changes to module items. Always call request_confirmation() first.

item_id.description: Module item ID from the index or fetch_module_items()

mcp_servers.name: canvas
mcp_servers._notes: DMontgomery40/mcp-canvas-lms running via docker-compose.yml. Fallback only when CANVAS_API_TOKEN is not set. When token IS set, Python-direct functions handle all reads and writes — MCP is not needed.

parameters._notes: Temperature 0.1 for tool-use and API operations. Raise to 0.5 only during recommendation narrative generation. stop_sequences closes the structured change plan block.

inputs.name: zip_path
inputs.format: Absolute file path to .imscc or .zip Canvas export

inputs.name: mode
inputs.notes: audit = no API calls; full = audit + propose + apply

inputs.name: CANVAS_API_TOKEN
inputs.required: required for full mode, not needed for audit mode

outputs.name: audit_report
outputs.format: {score: int, issues: [{rule_id, type, severity, module, item, description, recommendation}], summary: str}

outputs.name: change_plan
outputs.format: [{change_id, resource_type, resource_id, field, before, after, canvas_endpoint, rationale}]
outputs.conditions: Returned when issues are found and instructor requests changes

outputs.name: change_ledger
outputs.conditions: Returned after changes are applied in full mode

io_contract.side_effects: In full mode: modifies local extracted course files, creates .bak backups, and updates Canvas course resources via REST API.

primary_data.description: Cognitive load audit rules and BYUI course design standards used by analyze_cognitive_load() and fetch_byui_resources().

audit_rules.name: Module Item Count
audit_rules.recommendation: Split module into two sub-modules or move supporting materials to a Resources page outside the module flow. Target 5-7 items per module.

audit_rules.name: Missing Module Overview
audit_rules.condition: Module's first item is not a Page with 'overview' or 'intro' in the title
audit_rules.recommendation: Add an Overview page as the first item in every module. It should state: learning outcomes for the week, estimated time, what students will do, and how items connect.

audit_rules.name: Inconsistent Module Naming
audit_rules.condition: Module names do not follow a consistent pattern (e.g., 'Week X: Topic' or 'Unit X: Topic')
audit_rules.recommendation: Standardize module names to 'Week X: [Topic Name]' or 'Unit X: [Topic Name]'. Consistent naming reduces navigation time and sets expectations.

audit_rules.name: Duplicate Assignment Instructions
audit_rules.condition: Assignment description text is substantially duplicated in a linked Page item in the same module
audit_rules.recommendation: Remove the duplicate page and consolidate all instructions into the assignment itself. Students should find instructions in one place only.

audit_rules.name: Orphaned Pages
audit_rules.condition: Pages exist in the course that are not linked to any module
audit_rules.recommendation: Either add orphaned pages to the appropriate module or delete them. Students navigating via Modules will never reach these pages.

audit_rules.name: Missing Learning Outcomes on Overview
audit_rules.condition: Module overview page does not contain 'by the end' or 'you will be able to' language
audit_rules.recommendation: Add 2-4 measurable learning outcomes to every module overview page. Example: 'By the end of this week, you will be able to apply the Central Limit Theorem to real engineering problems.'

audit_rules.name: Missing Prove It Assessment
audit_rules.condition: Module contains no assignment or quiz item
audit_rules.recommendation: Every BYUI module should include a 'Prove It' assessment that lets students demonstrate mastery of the learning outcomes. Add an assignment or quiz.

audit_rules.name: Teach One Another Activity Missing
audit_rules.recommendation: BYUI's 'Teach One Another' model calls for peer interaction in each module. Consider adding a discussion activity where students apply or explain a concept to each other.

audit_rules.name: Excessive Page Text Length
audit_rules.condition: A single page contains more than 1500 words
audit_rules.recommendation: Break long pages into focused sub-pages (one concept per page) or convert dense text to a video or structured activity. Target <800 words per page for online courses.

audit_rules.name: Unpublished Items in Published Module
audit_rules.condition: Module is published but contains unpublished items — students will see a broken module flow
audit_rules.recommendation: Either publish all items in the module or move unpublished items to a draft module. Broken navigation links are a top source of student frustration.

canvas_api_endpoints.description: List all modules in the course with their published state and item count. Use during change plan phase to fetch module_ids; store results in .canvas/index.json so this call is not repeated.
List all items within a specific module. Use to fetch module_item positions before inserting; store results in .canvas/index.json.
Update module name, position, or published state. Use Python — MCP response returns full module object which is large.
canvas_api_endpoints.quirks: Rename only updates the module display name — if any module items also show the old name via their own title field, those must be updated separately.

canvas_api_endpoints.description: Update page title or body HTML. Use Python — response returns full page HTML body which is large.
canvas_api_endpoints.quirks: URL parameter must be the page slug (e.g. 'sprint-3-overview'), NOT the numeric page id. Slug is returned in the 'url' field of the create_page response — store it in .canvas/index.json immediately.

canvas_api_endpoints.description: Update assignment name, description, due date, or points. Use Python.
canvas_api_endpoints.quirks: Renaming an assignment does NOT rename the corresponding module item title — that must be updated separately via update_module_item.

canvas_api_endpoints.description: Create a new wiki page. Use Python. Immediately store the returned 'url' (slug) and 'page_id' in .canvas/index.json.
canvas_api_endpoints.quirks: Response includes 'url' field (the slug) — this is required for create_module_item when type=Page. Do not use the numeric id for module item insertion.

canvas_api_endpoints.description: Add a new item to a module. Use Python.
canvas_api_endpoints.quirks: For type=Page: use 'page_url' set to the slug (not 'content_id'). For type=Assignment or Quiz: use 'content_id' set to the numeric id. Mixing these causes 404 or wrong resource linked.

canvas_api_endpoints.description: Update a module item's title, position, or published state. Required when renaming an assignment or quiz — the module item title is independent of the resource name.
Create a classic quiz. Use Python.
canvas_api_endpoints.quirks: Do NOT set points_possible in the initial POST — it is unreliable. After creating the quiz and adding all questions, do a separate PUT to /courses/:id/quizzes/:quiz_id with quiz[points_possible] to set the correct total. Without this second call, the quiz and its linked gradebook assignment both show 0 points.

canvas_api_endpoints.description: Second required call after quiz creation to set points_possible correctly.
canvas_api_endpoints.quirks: This call must come AFTER all questions are added, not during creation. The linked assignment in the gradebook will reflect the correct total only after this PUT.

canvas_api_endpoints.description: List all pages in the course — used to detect orphaned pages and build the page slug cache in .canvas/index.json.
Get course metadata including enrollment counts and term dates. Safe via MCP — response is small.

persistent_index_schema._notes: Defines what .canvas/index.json should track per course. This is the schema only — actual data lives in .canvas/index.json alongside the course export. Rationale: canvas_course_expert.json is static agent config shared across all courses; the index is dynamic runtime state specific to one course instance.
persistent_index_schema.course_id: integer — Canvas course ID from the course URL
persistent_index_schema.last_audit: ISO8601 timestamp of most recent parse_course_export + analyze_cognitive_load run

modules._description: Keyed by module title for human readability

{module_title}.position: integer — current position in module list

pages._description: Keyed by page slug — slug is required for module item insertion

{assignment_title}.module_item_id: integer — needed to rename the module item independently

change_ledger._description: Persistent record of every write applied via this agent

{change_id}.rule_id: string — CL rule that triggered this change
{change_id}.canvas_id: integer — ID of the resource that was created or modified

byui_standards.standard: Standard BYUI module structure: (1) Overview page — outcomes, estimated time, connection to course goals; (2) Content items — readings, videos, or lectures; (3) Teach One Another — peer discussion or collaboration; (4) Prove It — individual assessment demonstrating mastery.
Modules should be named 'Week X: [Topic]' for weekly pacing or 'Unit X: [Topic]' for block pacing. Numbers should be zero-padded for proper sort order (Week 01, Week 02, ..., Week 10).

Every module should end with a 'Prove It' assessment — a task where students demonstrate they have mastered the module outcomes. Can be a quiz, assignment, lab, or project. Should be clearly labeled as the assessment item.

Each module should include a collaborative activity where students teach concepts to each other. This is typically a discussion forum prompt that requires students to explain, apply, or demonstrate a concept and respond to peers. Graded on substance, not participation.

Online courses must provide a single, clear path through the content. All required items must be in modules in sequence order. No required content should exist only in Files or Pages outside the module flow.
Each assignment and assessment should be explicitly linked to at least one course learning outcome. Outcome alignment should be visible to students on the assignment page.

environment_variables.name: CANVAS_API_TOKEN
environment_variables.description: Canvas API access token from Settings → Approved Integrations

environment_variables.name: CANVAS_COURSE_ID
environment_variables.description: Integer course ID visible in the Canvas course URL

environment_variables.name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
environment_variables.description: Anthropic API key for the LLM agent

pre_run_checklist: ZIP file exists and is readable
CANVAS_API_TOKEN, CANVAS_BASE_URL, CANVAS_COURSE_ID are set (full mode only)
Canvas API token has instructor-level write permission (test with GET /api/v1/courses/:id)
post_run_checklist: Audit report contains at least a score and summary
All applied changes have a corresponding .bak backup file
Change ledger shows no 'failed' status entries
Canvas API GET confirms all applied changes are live
success_criteria: parse_course_export returns course_data with at least one module
analyze_cognitive_load returns a score between 0 and 100
No canvas_api(POST/PUT/DELETE) calls made without change_plan confirmation
All local files updated before corresponding API calls

test_cases.name: Module item count flag
test_cases.input: Course with one module containing 10 items
test_cases.expected_output: CL-001 warning flagged for that module; recommendation to split

test_cases.name: Missing overview detection
test_cases.input: Module where first item is an assignment, not a page

test_cases.name: Orphaned page detection
test_cases.input: Course with 3 pages, only 2 linked in modules
test_cases.expected_output: CL-005 warning for the unlinked page with its title and path

test_cases.name: No API writes without confirmation
test_cases.input: Run full audit with issues found, do NOT confirm changes
test_cases.expected_output: Zero canvas_api POST/PUT/DELETE calls made; change_plan returned only

test_cases.name: BYUI fallback on login-gated page
test_cases.expected_output: Returns byui_standards['module_structure'] entry with source noted as embedded fallback

when_to_use: Before a new course goes live — run audit in audit-only mode as a pre-launch checklist
At the start of each semester — catch drift from BYUI standards caused by mid-semester edits
When a course has high drop rates or poor student satisfaction — use the cognitive load score as a diagnostic
After a major course redesign — validate the new structure before publishing
when_not_to_use: During an active exam window — never apply bulk changes while a timed assessment is in progress
When you don't have the course export ZIP — the agent cannot work from Canvas API reads alone (export required for full structural analysis)
For brand-new course builds — use the BYUI course design template instead; this agent is for auditing existing courses

best_practices.description: Run a full audit at least one week before a semester begins to allow time for changes to be reviewed and applied without rushing.
Temperature set to 0.1 for all tool calls. Canvas API errors from wrong resource IDs or malformed payloads are not easily reversible — determinism is required.
Log every tool call and its result. Canvas API write calls should log: endpoint, payload, response status, and timestamp. This creates an audit trail for change review.
Apply changes module-by-module rather than all at once. If a change fails mid-batch, only one module is affected and rollback is simpler.

known_failures.cause: Instructor exported a Canvas-native package instead of IMSCC Common Cartridge — imsmanifest.xml will not be found
known_failures.mitigation: Detect missing imsmanifest.xml and return format_error with instructions to re-export as Common Cartridge

known_failures.cause: Canvas enforces a 700 requests/minute rate limit; bulk operations can hit this
known_failures.mitigation: canvas_api_tool.py implements a token bucket rate limiter; backs off with exponential delay on 403 rate limit responses

known_failures.cause: Model repeatedly calls analyze_cognitive_load or fetch_byui_resources without converging on a change plan
known_failures.mitigation: Set max_turns=20 in the agent runner; tool calls are idempotent so repeated calls are safe but wasteful

known_failures.cause: Changes applied to a course with active enrolled students
known_failures.mitigation: Before any write operation, canvas_api_tool.py checks course enrollment count and term end date. If enrolled students > 0 and term end > today, warn before proceeding.

fallbacks.description: Return the matching entry from primary_data.byui_standards. Note the source as 'embedded knowledge base' in the recommendation.
Log the failed change with resource ID and endpoint. Do not continue applying other changes in the same batch — report to instructor for manual resolution.

prohibited_actions: Deleting any course content (no DELETE calls without explicit 'delete' keyword confirmation from instructor)
Modifying assignment due dates without instructor specifying the new date
Publishing or unpublishing the entire course
Modifying grade passback or external tool configurations

guardrails.description: Before any POST/PUT/DELETE canvas_api call, verify that the change appears in the approved change_plan and has status='approved'. Reject if not found.
guardrails.implementation: Checked in canvas_api_tool.py before every write call

guardrails.description: Before any write operation, fetch course enrollment count. If > 0 active students, display warning with student count and require additional confirmation.
Every local file write creates a .bak file. Every Canvas resource update logs the before-state in the change_ledger.
guardrails.implementation: Enforced in write_local_file() and canvas_api() wrapper

safety_boundaries.human_in_the_loop: Required before any write operation. Agent presents change_plan and halts. Instructor must explicitly confirm (all/select/none) before API calls proceed.

related_agents.name: doc_analysis_agent
related_agents.relationship: Could be used upstream to analyze course syllabi or external documents before ingesting them into Canvas pages

changes: 10 cognitive load audit rules covering extraneous, intrinsic, and germane load
6 embedded BYUI course design standards as fallback for login-gated teach.byui.edu content
5 tools: parse_course_export, canvas_api, read_local_file, write_local_file, analyze_cognitive_load, fetch_byui_resources
Human-in-the-loop guardrail before all write operations
Enrollment check guardrail before any live-course modifications
